messages.content:
Rank these, I'm overloaded and I keep re-reading the same four lines.

| Company | Title | Stage | Fit | Match | Days | Ghost risk | Docs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Loftwren | Senior Manager, Pricing and Analytics | interview | A- | 88% | 3 | low | yes |
| Sablewood Resorts Group | Director of Revenue Strategy | applied | A | 91% | 12 | medium | yes |
| Wanderfare Group | Senior Manager, Revenue Science | researched | B+ | 79% | 21 | medium | no |
| Northline Coach Company | Manager, Commercial Network Planning | sourced | C | 61% | 34 | high | no |

1. Loftwren — live interview, three days in, docs already tailored. Nothing outranks an active process.
2. Sablewood Resorts Group at 91% and grade A, but it's applied and twelve days quiet; medium ghost risk says nudge, not wait.
3. Wanderfare Group, 79% and B+, still researched at day 21 with no docs. Real, just cold.
4. Northline Coach Company: C fit, 61%, 34 days sourced, high ghost risk.
Drop Northline Coach Company. Every signal on that row points the same direction, and keeping it open is why the other three feel heavy.

What's actually worth my Saturday? Six things open, three hours, and I'd rather not waste it.
- Cinderpeak Resorts · Senior Vice President, Revenue and Distribution · conversation · B+ · 76% · 5 days · low · docs no
- Sablewood Resorts Group · Director of Revenue Strategy · applied · A- · 87% · 9 days · low · docs yes
- Loftwren · Senior Manager, Pricing and Analytics · sourced · A · 90% · 2 days · low · docs no
- Windrose Air Group · Manager, Network Planning · applied · B · 70% · 26 days · high · docs yes
- Wanderfare Group · Senior Manager, Revenue Science · researched · B · 72% · 15 days · medium · docs no
- Beacon Halloway Events · Supplier Performance Manager · sourced · C · 58% · 41 days · high · docs no

Saturday order, top to bottom:
1. Loftwren. A fit, 90%, two days old and no docs yet — freshness plus the highest match is where writing time pays.
2. Cinderpeak Resorts, because a conversation at five days is warm and missing docs are its only gap.
3. Sablewood Resorts Group — A-, 87%, low risk at nine days. Needs a nudge, not work.
4. Wanderfare Group over 5. Windrose Air Group; tiebreak: 72% researched beats 70% applied that's been silent 26 days at high risk.
6. Beacon Halloway Events — drop it. C, 58%, 41 days.

Quick one — three left after a brutal month, tell me the order and be blunt.
Windward Voyages / Vice President, Commercial / rejected / A- / 84% / 6 days / low / docs yes
Loftwren / Senior Manager, Pricing and Analytics / interview / B / 71% / 4 days / medium / docs yes
Saltmoor Expeditions / Director of Global Operations / researched / B+ / 80% / 11 days / low / docs no

Blunt version. Loftwren first — it's the only interview on the board, four days in, docs ready, and a B at 71% still beats a role you can't act on.
Saltmoor Expeditions second: B+, 80%, low ghost risk, and eleven days researched means it's ripe for an application you haven't written.
Windward Voyages third and last, because a rejected A- at 84% is history, not pipeline.
Nothing to drop. Three roles is already thin, and Windward Voyages costs nothing sitting there as a re-apply target if the role reposts.

Talk me out of the ones I'm attached to. I know I'm sentimental about Sablewood.
Role: Sablewood Resorts Group — Director of Revenue Strategy
stage: sourced | fit: A | match: 93% | days in stage: 47 | ghost risk: high | tailored docs: no
Role: Wanderfare Group — Senior Manager, Revenue Science
stage: applied | fit: B+ | match: 81% | days in stage: 7 | ghost risk: low | tailored docs: yes
Role: Cinderpeak Resorts — Senior Vice President, Revenue and Distribution
stage: conversation | fit: B | match: 68% | days in stage: 2 | ghost risk: low | tailored docs: no
Role: Northline Coach Company — Manager, Commercial Network Planning
stage: researched | fit: B | match: 74% | days in stage: 19 | ghost risk: medium | tailored docs: no
Role: Windrose Air Group — Manager, Network Planning
stage: sourced | fit: C | match: 60% | days in stage: 30 | ghost risk: high | tailored docs: no

Here's the argument you asked for.
Wanderfare Group ranks first: applied seven days ago, 81%, low risk, docs done. It's the only role with momentum.
Cinderpeak Resorts second — a two-day-old conversation beats anything untouched, even at 68%.
Northline Coach Company third on 74% and a medium risk that hasn't gone bad yet.
Sablewood Resorts Group fourth. Yes, A and 93%. It has also sat sourced 47 days with no docs at high ghost risk, which means you like it, not that it's moving.
Windrose Air Group last: C, 60%, 30 days. Drop that one. Sablewood stays only if you write its docs this week.

ok typing this fast between calls sorry for the mess. sablewood resorts group director of revenue strategy is applied 5 days A- 86% low risk docs yes. loftwren senior manager pricing and analytics conversation 8 days B+ 77% medium docs no. wanderfare group senior manager revenue science sourced 22 days A 89% medium docs no. cinderpeak resorts svp revenue and distribution rejected 3 days B 70% low docs yes. windrose air group manager network planning applied 31 days B 69% high docs yes. northline coach company manager commercial network planning researched 14 days C 62% medium docs no. beacon halloway events supplier performance manager sourced 55 days D 48% high docs no. rank all seven please

Seven roles, ranked:
1 — Sablewood Resorts Group. Applied five days ago, A-, 86%, docs done and risk low. Closest to a real outcome.
2 — Loftwren, an eight-day conversation at 77%; the missing docs are your weekend job.
3 — Wanderfare Group. Highest match at 89% and an A, but still sourced after 22 days, so it sits below two live processes.
4 — Windrose Air Group: 31 days applied, high risk, but docs are ready if they resurface.
5 — Northline Coach Company, 62% and stalled.
6 — Cinderpeak Resorts, rejected.
7 — Beacon Halloway Events: D, 48%, 55 days. Drop that one outright.

Which of these is dead, and what order do I work the rest in?
Loftwren | Senior Manager, Pricing and Analytics | offer | A | 94% | 2 days | low | docs yes
Sablewood Resorts Group | Director of Revenue Strategy | interview | A- | 88% | 6 days | low | docs yes
Wanderfare Group | Senior Manager, Revenue Science | applied | B+ | 78% | 18 days | medium | docs yes
Saltmoor Expeditions | Director of Global Operations | sourced | B | 66% | 39 days | high | docs no

An offer beats everything: Loftwren goes first — A, 94%, two days old, and offers expire.
Sablewood Resorts Group second. Interview at six days, A-, 88%, low risk; it's your leverage if the Loftwren number disappoints.
Wanderfare Group third, applied 18 days at 78% with docs already tailored, so following up costs you one message.
Saltmoor Expeditions fourth: 66%, sourced 39 days, high ghost risk, no docs.
Saltmoor Expeditions is the dead one — drop it. Nothing you haven't touched in 39 days survives contact with a live offer.

I keep opening this board and doing nothing. Force a ranking on me.
- Cinderpeak Resorts, Senior Vice President, Revenue and Distribution — interview, B, 72%, 9 days in stage, medium ghost risk, docs yes
- Loftwren, Senior Manager, Pricing and Analytics — interview, B+, 82%, 9 days in stage, medium ghost risk, docs yes
- Sablewood Resorts Group, Director of Revenue Strategy — researched, A, 92%, 4 days, low, docs no
- Windrose Air Group, Manager, Network Planning — applied, B, 73%, 25 days, high, docs yes
- Beacon Halloway Events, Supplier Performance Manager — sourced, C, 59%, 12 days, medium, docs no

Forced, no hedging.
First, Loftwren. Second, Cinderpeak Resorts. Both are interviews at nine days with identical medium risk and tailored docs — tiebreak: higher match wins, 82% over 72%.
Third is Sablewood Resorts Group, four days researched at 92% and grade A. Best role you own, and it needs documents rather than admiration.
Fourth, Windrose Air Group — 25 days applied, high risk, but docs are done so a follow-up is free.
Fifth, Beacon Halloway Events at 59%.
Drop Beacon Halloway Events. Nothing else goes yet; two live interviews earn the rest a week.

Three roles, 25 minutes before my shift. Order them and tell me what to cut.
Marrow Ridge Health · Director, Ambulatory Operations · applied · A- · 85% · 10 days · low · docs yes
Wexley Care Partners · Operations Manager, Home Infusion · conversation · B+ · 79% · 3 days · low · docs no
Verdigris Care Network · Senior Director, Quality and Regulatory Affairs · sourced · B · 68% · 28 days · high · docs no

Wexley Care Partners takes the top slot — a three-day-old conversation is the only thing here that decays if you ignore it, and it's B+ at 79% with no docs written.
Marrow Ridge Health is second: A-, 85%, tailored, ten days applied at low risk, so a short nudge is the whole task.
Verdigris Care Network is third by default — 68%, sourced 28 days, high ghost risk, nothing written.
Cut nothing today. Verdigris Care Network is the candidate, but you'd be down to two roles; give it until you've drafted its docs or it hits day 35.

My board's a mess after two rejections. Rank everything, don't spare me.

Company | Title | Stage | Fit | Match | Days | Ghost | Docs
Blue Heron Health Alliance | Chief Nursing Officer | rejected | B | 65% | 4 | low | yes
Harborlight Senior Living | Vice President of Clinical Operations | rejected | B+ | 74% | 8 | low | yes
Marrow Ridge Health | Director, Ambulatory Operations | interview | A | 90% | 5 | low | yes
Wexley Care Partners | Operations Manager, Home Infusion | applied | B | 71% | 16 | medium | yes
Verdigris Care Network | Senior Director, Quality and Regulatory Affairs | researched | A- | 83% | 6 | low | no
Larkfield Health System | Healthcare Data Analyst | sourced | C | 55% | 33 | high | no

Two rejections don't change the order much, because your best role is still live.
1. Marrow Ridge Health — interview, A, 90%, five days, docs ready.
2. Verdigris Care Network. A- at 83%, six days researched, low risk; write the docs and it becomes your second live process.
3. Wexley Care Partners, applied 16 days at 71% with tailored docs, waiting on a reply.
4. Larkfield Health System — 55% and 33 days sourced, but the only unworked role left.
5. Harborlight Senior Living, 6. Blue Heron Health Alliance; tiebreak among rejections: higher match first, 74% over 65%.
Drop Larkfield Health System the moment Verdigris moves.

no labels, you know the columns:
Wexley Care Partners, Operations Manager, Home Infusion, interview, B, 70%, 2, medium, yes
Marrow Ridge Health, Director, Ambulatory Operations, applied, A, 91%, 21, high, yes
Verdigris Care Network, Senior Director, Quality and Regulatory Affairs, conversation, B+, 77%, 9, low, no
Harborlight Senior Living, Vice President of Clinical Operations, sourced, A-, 86%, 3, low, no
what's the order

Order: Wexley Care Partners, Verdigris Care Network, Harborlight Senior Living, Marrow Ridge Health.
Wexley leads on stage alone — interview, two days in, docs ready, and a B at 70% you can still convert.
Verdigris Care Network next: a nine-day conversation at 77% with no docs written is the clearest unit of work on the board.
Harborlight Senior Living third, because A- and 86% at three days sourced is fresh, cheap upside.
Marrow Ridge Health last despite A and 91% — 21 days applied at high ghost risk means the decision isn't yours anymore.
Drop nothing. One follow-up to Marrow Ridge, then let it stand or fall.

Eight open. I know that's too many. Rank all of them and tell me which two to kill.
1) Marrow Ridge Health — Director, Ambulatory Operations — researched — A — 89% — 7d — low — docs no
2) Wexley Care Partners — Operations Manager, Home Infusion — applied — B+ — 78% — 12d — medium — docs yes
3) Verdigris Care Network — Senior Director, Quality and Regulatory Affairs — sourced — B — 69% — 26d — medium — docs no
4) Harborlight Senior Living — Vice President of Clinical Operations — interview — A- — 84% — 4d — low — docs yes
5) Blue Heron Health Alliance — Chief Nursing Officer — sourced — C — 57% — 44d — high — docs no
6) Larkfield Health System — Healthcare Data Analyst — sourced — D — 46% — 51d — high — docs no
7) Emberline Health — Clinical Content Lead — conversation — B — 72% — 6d — low — docs no
8) Osterby Clinical Communications — Associate Medical Writer — researched — B — 66% — 18d — medium — docs no

Ranking, then the two kills.
1 Harborlight Senior Living — interview, 4 days, A-, docs ready.
2 Marrow Ridge Health — A, 89%, researched a week. Write the docs.
3 Emberline Health — six-day conversation, still warm at 72%.
4 Wexley Care Partners — applied 12 days, 78%, docs done, needs a nudge.
5 Osterby Clinical Communications ahead of 6 Verdigris Care Network; tiebreak: both B, fresher stage wins, 18 days researched over 26 days sourced.
7 Blue Heron Health Alliance, 57% at 44 days.
8 Larkfield Health System, D and 46%.
Kill Larkfield Health System and Blue Heron Health Alliance. Neither has moved in six weeks.

Honest question — am I holding on to dead weight? Rank these five.
company: Verdigris Care Network / title: Senior Director, Quality and Regulatory Affairs / stage: interview / fit: B+ / match: 80% / days: 11 / ghost: medium / docs: yes
company: Marrow Ridge Health / title: Director, Ambulatory Operations / stage: conversation / fit: A / match: 92% / days: 5 / ghost: low / docs: no
company: Wexley Care Partners / title: Operations Manager, Home Infusion / stage: rejected / fit: B / match: 73% / days: 2 / ghost: low / docs: yes
company: Harborlight Senior Living / title: Vice President of Clinical Operations / stage: applied / fit: A- / match: 87% / days: 30 / ghost: high / docs: yes
company: Emberline Health / title: Clinical Content Lead / stage: sourced / fit: C / match: 54% / days: 22 / ghost: medium / docs: no

Yes, some. Here's the order.
Marrow Ridge Health leads: A, 92%, a five-day conversation at low ghost risk. Best role, warmest moment, and it has no tailored docs — that's your next hour.
Verdigris Care Network second, an active interview at 80%, eleven days in, medium risk. Prep beats prospecting.
Harborlight Senior Living third: 87% and A-, but 30 days applied at high risk is a nudge, not a plan.
Emberline Health fourth at 54%.
Wexley Care Partners fifth, rejected.
Drop Emberline Health. It's the only row where fit, match, and stage all say no at once.

Second week of not knowing where to start. Give me a ranked list, one line each.
Harborlight Senior Living — Vice President of Clinical Operations — researched, B+, 76%, 13 days, medium, no docs
Osterby Clinical Communications — Associate Medical Writer — applied, B, 64%, 4 days, low, docs yes
Marrow Ridge Health — Director, Ambulatory Operations — sourced, A, 90%, 9 days, low, no docs
Verdigris Care Network — Senior Director, Quality and Regulatory Affairs — applied, A-, 85%, 20 days, high, docs yes

Top: Marrow Ridge Health. A and 90% at only nine days sourced is the highest-value unwritten application you have.
Then Verdigris Care Network — A-, 85%, docs already tailored, and 20 days at high ghost risk makes the follow-up overdue rather than optional.
Third, Harborlight Senior Living: B+ at 76%, thirteen days researched, no docs.
Fourth, Osterby Clinical Communications — applied four days ago, but 64% and B make it the weakest thing you're waiting on.
Nothing gets dropped this week. The board is four roles deep; your problem is unwritten docs, not clutter.

Rank these four for me. I have one evening of energy, not four.
· Cadenza Payments · Staff Software Engineer, Core Ledger · interview · A · 93% · 3 days · low · docs yes
· Alderport Financial · Principal Engineer, Payments Platform · applied · A- · 88% · 14 days · medium · docs yes
· Halberd Commerce · Engineering Manager, Payments · researched · B · 70% · 8 days · low · docs no
· Prairiestone Credit Group · Data Analyst I, Lending · sourced · D · 41% · 36 days · high · docs no

One evening, one place to spend it: Cadenza Payments. Interview, three days in, A at 93%, docs done — prep the ledger questions and stop there.
Alderport Financial is second. Fourteen days applied at 88% with medium risk earns exactly one follow-up message, not an evening.
Halberd Commerce ranks third: B, 70%, eight days researched, no docs. It's a fallback, and a manager role is a different job than the two above it.
Prairiestone Credit Group is fourth and gone. D, 41%, 36 days untouched — drop it tonight so it stops counting as a task.

Six on the board, two of them I only added because a recruiter messaged me. Rank all six.

| co | role | stage | fit | match | days | ghost | docs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Alderport Financial | Principal Engineer, Payments Platform | interview | A- | 87% | 6 | low | yes |
| Cadenza Payments | Staff Software Engineer, Core Ledger | interview | B+ | 81% | 6 | low | yes |
| Halberd Commerce | Engineering Manager, Payments | conversation | B | 74% | 11 | medium | no |
| Ardent Ledger Group | Senior Manager, Model Risk Governance | sourced | C | 58% | 19 | high | no |
| Northfell Capital Partners | Vice President, Risk Analytics | sourced | C | 56% | 19 | high | no |
| Cordwain Financial | Analyst, Portfolio Reporting | researched | B | 63% | 25 | medium | no |

Both interviews sit at six days with docs ready, so: 1 Alderport Financial, 2 Cadenza Payments — tiebreak: higher fit grade, A- over B+.
3 Halberd Commerce, an eleven-day conversation at 74% that will cool if you leave it another week.
4 Cordwain Financial — 63% and B, researched 25 days, medium risk. Weak, but at least you looked at it.
5 Ardent Ledger Group, 6 Northfell Capital Partners; tiebreak on match, 58% over 56%, since every other column on those two rows is identical.
Drop both recruiter adds. C fit at 19 days sourced with high ghost risk is someone else's pipeline, not yours.

short board, long week. cadenza payments staff software engineer core ledger, offer, A, 95%, 1 day, low, docs yes. alderport financial principal engineer payments platform, interview, A-, 86%, 7 days, low, docs yes. halberd commerce engineering manager payments, applied, B, 68%, 23 days, high, docs no. order?

Cadenza Payments, obviously — an offer at day one, A, 95%. Everything else waits until you've read the terms.
Alderport Financial second, and it matters: an A- interview at 86%, seven days in, is the only thing that gives you a second number to compare against. Tell them an offer is live and ask to compress the remaining rounds.
Halberd Commerce third — 68%, B, 23 days applied, high ghost risk, docs never written.
Drop Halberd Commerce now. With an offer on the table you will not go back and tailor documents for your weakest role.

Seven roles. Tell me which are dead and rank the survivors in the same list.
Cadenza Payments :: Staff Software Engineer, Core Ledger :: applied :: A :: 90% :: 9d :: low :: docs yes
Alderport Financial :: Principal Engineer, Payments Platform :: researched :: A- :: 85% :: 3d :: low :: docs no
Halberd Commerce :: Engineering Manager, Payments :: rejected :: B+ :: 79% :: 5d :: low :: docs yes
Ardent Ledger Group :: Senior Manager, Model Risk Governance :: conversation :: B :: 72% :: 17d :: medium :: docs no
Stonebrook Credit :: Director of Credit Risk :: sourced :: C :: 52% :: 40d :: high :: docs no
Cordwain Financial :: Analyst, Portfolio Reporting :: sourced :: D :: 44% :: 48d :: high :: docs no
Ashgrove Mutual Insurance :: Associate Pricing Analyst :: sourced :: C :: 50% :: 29d :: medium :: docs no

Survivors first, dead at the bottom, one list.
1 Cadenza Payments — nine days applied, A, 90%, docs tailored. Your best live shot.
2 Alderport Financial — three days researched, A-, 85%. Newest strong role; write and send.
3 Ardent Ledger Group — the conversation is 17 days old and cooling, but 72% is real.
4 Ashgrove Mutual Insurance — 50%, 29 days; tiebreak against Stonebrook: lower ghost risk wins.
5 Stonebrook Credit — 52% but 40 days and high risk.
6 Cordwain Financial — D, 44%, 48 days.
7 Halberd Commerce — rejected.
Dead: Cordwain Financial and Stonebrook Credit. Drop both.

Talk me out of the two I'm emotionally attached to, if you can. Ranking please.
role=Alderport Financial | Principal Engineer, Payments Platform
stage=sourced fit=A match=94% days=52 ghost=high docs=no
role=Cadenza Payments | Staff Software Engineer, Core Ledger
stage=interview fit=B+ match=80% days=8 ghost=medium docs=yes
role=Halberd Commerce | Engineering Manager, Payments
stage=applied fit=B match=71% days=6 ghost=low docs=yes
role=Northfell Capital Partners | Vice President, Risk Analytics
stage=researched fit=B+ match=77% days=12 ghost=low docs=no
role=Ardent Ledger Group | Senior Manager, Model Risk Governance
stage=sourced fit=C match=55% days=33 ghost=high docs=no

The attachment is to Alderport Financial, and 94% is a real number — but it has been sourced for 52 days with no docs at high ghost risk. A role you haven't applied to isn't a role. It ranks third.
Above it: Cadenza Payments first, an eight-day interview at 80% with docs ready. Halberd Commerce second, applied six days ago at 71%, low risk, already tailored.
Fourth, Northfell Capital Partners — B+, 77%, twelve days researched.
Fifth, Ardent Ledger Group at 55%.
Drop Ardent Ledger Group. Give Alderport Financial one week: apply, or it goes too.

Everything here is actually going okay for once, which is its own problem — where do I put the hours?
Cadenza Payments / Staff Software Engineer, Core Ledger / interview / A / 92% / 4 days / low / docs yes
Alderport Financial / Principal Engineer, Payments Platform / interview / B+ / 78% / 4 days / low / docs yes
Halberd Commerce / Engineering Manager, Payments / conversation / A- / 84% / 2 days / low / docs no
Ardent Ledger Group / Senior Manager, Model Risk Governance / applied / B / 69% / 5 days / low / docs yes

Good problem. Hours go here:
Cadenza Payments first — A, 92%, interview at four days. Deepest prep, most upside.
Halberd Commerce second, ahead of the other interview, because a two-day-old conversation at A- and 84% with no docs is the one thing that can still be lost by inaction.
Alderport Financial third: same stage, same four days, but 78% and B+ make it the cheaper prep.
Ardent Ledger Group fourth — applied five days ago at 69%, docs done, nothing to do but wait.
Drop nothing. Every role is under a week old at low ghost risk; a healthy board is not a cluttered one.

Mostly rejections. I think I need to rebuild, but rank what's left first.
Cadenza Payments — Staff Software Engineer, Core Ledger — rejected — A — 91% — 3 days — low — docs yes
Halberd Commerce — Engineering Manager, Payments — rejected — B+ — 76% — 9 days — low — docs yes
Ardent Ledger Group — Senior Manager, Model Risk Governance — rejected — B — 70% — 14 days — low — docs yes
Alderport Financial — Principal Engineer, Payments Platform — researched — A- — 87% — 2 days — low — docs no
Northfell Capital Partners — Vice President, Risk Analytics — sourced — B — 66% — 5 days — medium — docs no
Prairiestone Credit Group — Data Analyst I, Lending — sourced — C — 49% — 11 days — high — docs no

Only three of these are pipeline, so rank them accordingly.
Alderport Financial is first by a wide margin — A-, 87%, researched two days ago, docs unwritten. That's tonight.
Northfell Capital Partners second: 66% and B, five days sourced, medium risk. Thin, but new.
Prairiestone Credit Group third at 49% with high ghost risk after eleven days.
Then the rejections, ordered by what's worth re-approaching later: Cadenza Payments fourth at 91%, Halberd Commerce fifth at 76%, Ardent Ledger Group sixth at 70%.
Drop Prairiestone Credit Group. You're right about rebuilding — one strong role and one weak one is not a board.

Board review before I lose the evening. Rank all five, then tell me what dies.
Hollandale Industries | Vice President of Manufacturing Operations | applied | A | 90% | 11 | low | docs yes
Brightfield Provisions | Plant Manager | interview | B+ | 79% | 5 | low | docs yes
Palisade Converting | Production Manager, Packaging | researched | B | 68% | 16 | medium | docs no
Brightloom Foods | Continuous Improvement Manager | sourced | C | 57% | 24 | high | docs no
Kestrel Precision Components | Quality Engineer, Machining | sourced | D | 43% | 38 | high | docs no

Brightfield Provisions is number one — the only interview, five days in, B+ at 79%, docs ready. A live process wins.
Hollandale Industries takes second: A and 90% is the best fit you have, but at eleven days applied the ball is theirs.
Palisade Converting third, 68% and sixteen days researched with nothing written.
Brightloom Foods fourth at 57%.
Kestrel Precision Components fifth — D, 43%, 38 days, and a step down in scope from everything above it.
Kestrel Precision Components dies today. Brightloom Foods gets until day 30, then it goes too.

3 roles. rank + cut call. quick.
Hollandale Industries, Vice President of Manufacturing Operations, conversation, A-, 88%, 6, low, no
Brightfield Provisions, Plant Manager, applied, B, 72%, 19, medium, yes
Delderfield Foods, Controls Engineer, sourced, D, 40%, 45, high, no

Hollandale Industries, then Brightfield Provisions, then Delderfield Foods.
Hollandale leads because a six-day conversation at 88% and A- is warm and the docs still aren't written — that gap is the entire risk.
Brightfield Provisions is nineteen days applied at 72% with docs done, so it costs one follow-up and no more.
Delderfield Foods is 40%, D, untouched for 45 days, and it sits well below the level of the other two.
Cut Delderfield Foods. Two roles is a thin board, but a fake third doesn't fix that.

Seven. Some of these I can't remember adding. Full ranking please.
Hollandale Industries | Vice President of Manufacturing Operations
  stage: interview, fit: A, match: 93%, days: 8, ghost: medium, docs: yes
Brightfield Provisions | Plant Manager
  stage: interview, fit: A, match: 93%, days: 15, ghost: medium, docs: yes
Palisade Converting | Production Manager, Packaging
  stage: applied, fit: B+, match: 80%, days: 7, ghost: low, docs: yes
Brightloom Foods | Continuous Improvement Manager
  stage: conversation, fit: B, match: 74%, days: 21, ghost: high, docs: no
Vandry Surgical | Supplier Quality Engineer II
  stage: researched, fit: C, match: 60%, days: 30, ghost: medium, docs: no
Halbrook Stamping Works | Maintenance and Automation Supervisor
  stage: sourced, fit: C, match: 55%, days: 42, ghost: high, docs: no
Delderfield Foods | Controls Engineer
  stage: sourced, fit: D, match: 47%, days: 50, ghost: high, docs: no

Hollandale Industries and Brightfield Provisions are identical on paper — A, 93%, interview, medium risk, docs done. Tiebreak: fresher stage wins, so Hollandale at 8 days ranks first and Brightfield at 15 days second.
Third, Palisade Converting: applied a week ago at 80%, low risk, tailored.
Fourth, Brightloom Foods — the conversation is 21 days old at high risk, which is nearly dead, but 74% keeps it alive for one more message.
Fifth Vandry Surgical, sixth Halbrook Stamping Works, seventh Delderfield Foods.
Drop the bottom three. None has been touched in a month and all sit below your level.

I've got a plant walk in an hour and this is nagging me. Rank, and be direct about what I should stop carrying.
- Brightfield Provisions · Plant Manager · offer · B+ · 78% · 4 days · low · docs yes
- Hollandale Industries · Vice President of Manufacturing Operations · interview · A · 94% · 2 days · low · docs yes
- Palisade Converting · Production Manager, Packaging · applied · B · 70% · 27 days · high · docs yes
- Brightloom Foods · Continuous Improvement Manager · sourced · C · 58% · 35 days · high · docs no

Two things matter and two don't.
Hollandale Industries ranks first even though Brightfield Provisions holds the offer — A at 94%, interview, two days in, and it's the bigger role. Move it fast enough to compare.
Brightfield Provisions second: an offer at 78% is real leverage and it has a clock on it.
Palisade Converting third, 27 days applied at high risk.
Brightloom Foods fourth at 58% after 35 days sourced.
Stop carrying Brightloom Foods. Palisade Converting stays one more week only because its documents are already tailored and a follow-up costs you nothing.

typed in the truck, sorry. hollandale industries Vice President of Manufacturing Operations researched A 91% 5 days low no docs. brightfield provisions plant manager applied B+ 82% 13 days medium docs yes. palisade converting production manager packaging conversation B 75% 4 days low no docs. brightloom foods continuous improvement manager applied B 69% 22 days high docs yes. kestrel precision components quality engineer machining sourced C 54% 31 days medium no docs. vandry surgical supplier quality engineer II rejected C 59% 6 days low docs yes. rank them all

1. Palisade Converting — four-day conversation at 75%, low risk, no docs. Warm and unfinished, so it's first.
2. Hollandale Industries — A, 91%, five days researched. Highest ceiling on the board and one writing session from being live.
3. Brightfield Provisions — 82%, thirteen days applied, docs tailored. Send the follow-up.
4. Brightloom Foods — 69% and 22 days applied at high ghost risk. One message, then let it go.
5. Kestrel Precision Components — 54%, 31 days sourced, below your level.
6. Vandry Surgical — rejected.
Drop Kestrel Precision Components. Brightloom Foods follows it out if that message goes unanswered.

This board is all top of funnel and I know it. Rank anyway.
Hollandale Industries — Vice President of Manufacturing Operations — sourced — A — 92% — 3 days — low — no docs
Brightfield Provisions — Plant Manager — sourced — A- — 86% — 6 days — low — no docs
Palisade Converting — Production Manager, Packaging — sourced — B+ — 77% — 10 days — medium — no docs
